verify.rule: Ensure all required fields are present.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances (+/-0.05mm).

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient thermal management leading to component failure.
risk.mit: Increase heat dissipation surface area and consider additional cooling solutions.

risk.desc: Deployment mechanism may not function correctly if rail guides are not precise.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough deployment tests and refine rail guide dimensions.

risk.desc: Insufficient service access could hinder maintenance and inspection.
risk.mit: Design larger and more accessible service panels.

risk.desc: Electrical shorts may occur if harness passthroughs are not properly insulated.
risk.mit: Use proper insulation materials and conduct electrical continuity tests.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)
physics_paths: Load Path: Structural loads from solar panels and antennas are transferred through the frame.
Flow Path: Electrical signals travel along the PC/104 stack and through harness passthroughs.
Thermal Path: Heat generated by electronics is dissipated through the aluminum frame.
Motion Path: Deployable rail guides allow for the extension of solar panels and antennas.
Pressure Path: No significant pressure differentials expected within the CubeSat.
Human/Service Access Path: Service access is provided via removable panels for maintenance and inspection.

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present.
verify.rule: part_tree/geometry_ops

risk.desc: Return path and grounding implicit, may lead to electromagnetic interference issues.
risk.mit: Specify grounding scheme explicitly and ensure signal returns are routed alongside signals.

risk.desc: Thermal management not fully specified, could affect hinge mechanism performance.
risk.mit: Define thermal management strategy and incorporate thermal dissipation features.

risk.desc: Potential strain on cables due to lack of detailed bend radius specifications.
risk.mit: Specify minimum bend radii for all cable exits and ensure routing avoids sharp edges.

physics_paths.description: Stowage and deployment loads transferred from hinge to antenna slot
RF signal flow from connector to board
Thermal management from sun exposure to hinge mechanism
Mechanical motion from hinge to antenna slot
Human service access for RF connector

integration_opportunities.details: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.
